_comment: Type E — Constraint Satisfaction / Deductive Closure (Q17-style / Jun's programming course). No fallacy. No implicit assumptions. All edges are deductive.
source_text: The programming course has two entry rules. If an applicant already has programming knowledge, they only need to pass the mathematics test. If an applicant has no prior programming knowledge, they must either achieve an excellent grade in mathematics, or pass both the mathematics and the chess tests. Jun is an experienced programmer. She sat the mathematics test but did not pass it.

premises.text: If an applicant has programming knowledge, they only need to pass the mathematics test. (HasKnowledge → OnlyNeedPassMaths)

If an applicant has no programming knowledge, they must either achieve an excellent grade in mathematics, or pass both mathematics and chess. (¬HasKnowledge → ExcellentMaths ∨ (PassMaths ∧ PassChess))
Jun is an experienced programmer — she has programming knowledge.
Jun sat the mathematics test but did not pass it.

conclusion.text: Jun cannot be accepted onto the programming course.

sub_conclusions.text: Because Jun has programming knowledge (P3), she only needed to pass mathematics (from P1).
